title: SSM and Doppler conflate permanent and transient errors
description: Both backends map auth/permission failures to SecretError variants that don't distinguish them from transient errors. SSM: AccessDeniedException and KMS decryption failure both map to Unavailable alongside throttling and network errors. Doppler: 401 (bad token) maps to Backend alongside 5xx server errors. Callers who implement retry loops (or document 'retry on Unavailable') will infinitely retry permission failures that will never succeed. This will generate user issues from anyone with IAM or token misconfiguration — the error message gives no indication that retry is futile.
